     Load-bearing code claims independently spot-checked against live files:
     legacy layer dead (index.html), schedule-renderer.js:363 merge, detectTrackChanges mutation
     (let mutated is :262 not :266), track-changes-store.js hide()/clamp, GridCoords.fromEvent. All verified. -->

# Spårplanen — Cut & Attach Editing: Design Research Document

*Lead designer's definitive recommendation for the dispatch-board editing feature. Written for the solo owner. Every claim below is grounded in the verified codebase; load-bearing facts were re-checked against the live files (`schedule-renderer.js:363` merge point, `expandSplitTrains:566` reading only `splitTracks`, the `detectTrackChanges` `forEach`, `GridCoords.fromEvent` shape, `UserTrainsStore` keys, `TrackChangesStore.hide` at `:122-134`, `vehicleTypes.js`).*

> **Correctness note on the suppression primitive.** An earlier draft asserted that `TrackChangesStore.hide(train.id)` "matches on `train.id` directly, covering all legs of that record." **That is false in the dominant case and has been corrected throughout.** Verified against `track-changes-store.js:122-134`: `hide()` only covers a record's legs when matching change entries *already exist* in the in-memory store (the `entry.trainId === k` branch). For a manual re-track where the API never reported a divergence — which is the *dominant* case, and precisely *why* the controller is overriding — `Array.from(changes.values()).filter(...)` returns empty, `hide()` falls into the `matching.length === 0` branch, and merely adds the bare `train.id` string to the `hidden` Set. That orphan entry suppresses nothing: future change entries are keyed by the composite `changeId` `${train.id}:${leg}:${trainNumber}`, and both `getActive()` (`:107`) and `isHidden()` (`:148-149`) test `hidden.has(entry.changeId)`, never the bare `train.id` against a composite key. So a *pre-emptive* `hide(train.id)` on a clean train is a **silent no-op** against any change the poll records 30 s later. Worse, the auto-unhide in `recordChange()` (`:86`, `if (hidden.has(k)) hidden.delete(k)`) only fires when `k` equals the recorded `changeId`, which a bare `train.id` key never is — so the orphan leaks forever. **The load-bearing suppression in this design is therefore the `manualOverride` gate inside `detectTrackChanges` (§5), not the `hide()` call.** `hide()` is used only to clear indicators for changes that *already exist*, and is called *after* a change is recorded (or replaced by a per-train `suppress(trainId)` — see §5 step 2).

---

## 1. Executive summary & recommendation

Build an **explicit, bounded edit mode** — the pen toggle is the right idea and you should ship it, because a wrong track assignment on a live 196-bar safety board is an operational hazard and the bounded session conveniently triples as the **undo scope**, the **audit boundary**, and the **live-feed-freeze window**. Inside that mode, graft the power-user keyboard layer (number-key track-jump, arrow-key nudge, Tab-cycle attach targeting) so the mode never feels heavy for a fast repetitive re-track — that keyboard layer is *also* your mandatory WCAG 2.5.7 non-drag path, so it pays for itself twice. Model every gesture as a **railway operation over the arrival↔departure pairing** (vändning, spårändring, koppling, delning), not free-form pixel art, and persist edits as an **append-only op log over the immutable base**, keyed by a **stable natural key** (week|day|track|subTrack|number@time) — never the volatile record `id`, which renumbers every weekly regen and would silently re-bind your edits to unrelated trains. Apply the log as a **projection in `prepareTrainData`** right after the `UserTrainsStore` merge (`schedule-renderer.js:363`); `trains.js` stays read-only.

**Verdict on the pen-icon idea: adopt it, exactly as you sketched (pen → work → Slutför), with three explicit exit doors (Slutför / Avbryt / Esc-with-confirm).** Do *not* relayout on toggle — reveal affordances in place.

**Verdict on track-change suppression: pin-and-override-with-an-escape-hatch, never a silent hide — and the suppression must be the `detectTrackChanges` gate, not `TrackChangesStore.hide`.** A manual re-track sets a `manualOverride` flag that `detectTrackChanges` reads on **every poll**: it **suppresses the auto-switch mutation** (so the 30 s poll stops fighting you) while **still running the API-vs-`plannedTrackId` compare** (so a *genuinely new* divergence is still detectable). `hide()` only clears the visible indicator for a change that has actually been recorded; on a clean train it does nothing, so it cannot be the suppression mechanism. A dismissible toast ("Live spårändringar avstängda för tåg X — Följ live igen") explains the state; the "don't show again" lives on the *toast*, never on the suppression. Keep `plannedTrackId` frozen so a *genuinely later* API divergence still surfaces as a "Live disagrees" chip rather than being swallowed.

Ship localStorage-first (matches every existing store); Firestore last-write-wins-with-history is a clean phase 2.

---

## 2. What "cut & attach" actually means here

### 2.1 The domain mapped onto the data model

The single most important insight, confirmed against `extract_monday.py:731-768` and the record shape at the head of `trains.js`: **one record IS one physical unit's dwell on one `(trackId, subTrackIndex)` lane, and it already fuses an arrival movement with a departure movement.** The left edge (`x0` / `scheduledArrivalTime`) carries `arrivalTrainNumber` — the train that *brought the unit in*. The right edge (`x1` / `scheduledDepartureTime`) carries `departureTrainNumber` — the train it *leaves as*. When those two numbers differ, the unit has already turned (**vändning**) — and that turn is baked into the one record; there is **no cross-record link** for a turn.

This means the controller's verbatim ask — "cut train bars and attach them to others" — is, in data terms, almost entirely **re-pairing arrivals with departures plus track/time moves**, not geometry editing. The five real-world Göteborg C operations map cleanly:

| Railway operation (Swedish) | Meaning | Data dimension touched |
|---|---|---|
| **Vändning** (turnback) | Arriving unit departs as a *new* train number | `arrivalTrainNumber` ≠ `departureTrainNumber` on one record (already encoded) |
| **Spårändring / spårbyte** | Platform/track reassignment | `trackId` / `subTrackIndex` |
| **Koppling / förstärkning** | Join/strengthen units | `trainSet.count` ↑ |
| **Delning / försvagning** | Split/weaken units | `trainSet.count` ↓, fan out to N departures |
| **Växling** (shunting) | Connective non-revenue move | already `kind:'shunting'` records |

### 2.2 The concrete edit operations

Each operation below names its **data transform**, its **railway meaning**, and the dead-`historyEngine` **action-type vocabulary** we reuse as a naming convention (the engine is dead code — names only).

**CUT is genuinely four distinct transforms** (a bar fuses several things), disambiguated by *where on the bar* the cut lands — a left/right-third vs middle **zone** scheme. This **extends the left/right-*half* hit-test pattern** at `train-renderer.js:290-300` — but note honestly that the precedent is weaker than a direct reuse: that existing logic is a **binary left/right half** test (`(e.clientX - rect.left) >= rect.width/2`) that only swaps which tooltip number shows; it has **no notion of zones**. The thirds/middle zoning here is **net-new**. Critically, **`rect.width` is unreliable for edge-clamped / overnight bars** (the §4.2 rule), so zone inference **must use `arrTime`/`depTime`-derived positions** (the inverse of the time→x formula), never the pixel `rect.width`, for any bar near a canvas edge or crossing midnight.

| Cut variant | Where (zone) | Transform | Railway meaning | Verb |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Sever the turn** | left/right third | One `through` record → an *arrival-only* stub (keeps `arrivalTrainNumber`, blanks `departure*`, `movementKind→'arrival'`) + a loose *departure-only* segment (`movementKind→'departure'`) whose `departureTrainNumber` is now re-pairable | Decouple the onward leg so the unit can become a different departure | `TRAIN_SPLIT` |
| **Time-split** | middle | One dwell → two consecutive same-lane dwells at `cutTime` | Park-then-reuse: one occupancy becomes two | `TRAIN_SPLIT` |
| **Decouple a unit** | on a `count>1` bar | Lower `trainSet.count`, emit a sibling bar | isärkoppling / försvagning | `TRAIN_SPLIT` |
| **Delete one leg** | per-leg | Drop the arrival or departure half | Cancellation of one movement | `TRAIN_DELETE` |

**ATTACH is the inverse — also several transforms**, chosen by *where on the target* you drop:

| Attach variant | Target | Transform | Railway meaning | Verb |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Form a turn (re-pair)** | arrival/departure edge of bar B | Write B's `departureTrainNumber`/`Label`/`depTime` onto the arriving record | vändning / change-of-turn | `TRAIN_SWAP` |
| **Couple** | body of compatible bar at same window | Raise `trainSet.count` on the target; consume the dragged segment | koppling / förstärkning | `TRAIN_EDIT` |
| **Stitch time-segments** | empty lane abutting B in time | Re-join two consecutive same-lane dwells (inverse of time-split) | merge two occupancies | `TRAIN_SPLIT` (inverse) |

**The three independent move dimensions** (kept as separate gestures, because `historyEngine` already separates them and they really are orthogonal):

- **Re-track** = change `trackId` and/or `subTrackIndex` — `TRAIN_MOVE`. Validate against closures + `isTrackSuitableForVehicleType`.
- **Re-time** = change `scheduledArrivalTime` / `scheduledDepartureTime` — `TRAIN_RESIZE`.
- **Re-pair** = change which numbers the bar fuses — `TRAIN_SWAP` / `TRAIN_SPLIT` (the cut/attach above).

**Couple (K) and turn (J) are deliberately kept as distinct verbs.** They are physically different operations — coupling raises `trainSet.count` on *one* bar (widening the lane span used by both `_packLanes` and `conflict-detector`), while a turn re-pairs *two numbers* on one bar with no count change. The external research explicitly warns against conflating them, and the data math diverges.

---

## 3. The existing architecture you will build on

The single biggest correction the research surfaced, and the one that reshapes the whole feature: **the legacy interaction/persistence/history layer is dead code.** `index.html` loads *none* of `events/*`, `historyEngine.js`, `persistenceEngine.js`, `modalManager.js`, or `menuSystem.js` (verified — zero `<script src>` references). `events/mouseEvents.js` contains a complete mousedown drag/resize/swap implementation and `historyEngine.js` a full undo/redo — **both are reference material, not a foundation.** Do not revive them. There is no working undo/redo, no drag-edit, and no edit-persistence in the live app today.

### Reuse map (active code paths)

| Concern | Reuse | File / symbol |
|---|---|---|
| Pointer → (track, subTrack, snapped 5-min time) | **`GridCoords.fromEvent(e)` → `{trackId, subTrackIndex, time, snappedTime, x, y}`** | `js/components/grid-actions/grid-coords.js` (`SNAP_MINUTES=5`) |
| Bar edge hit-testing (px proximity only) | `getBarMetrics(bar)` | `track-changes-decorator.js:83` |
| Overlay store pattern to clone | **`UserTrainsStore`** — IIFE, localStorage (`sparplannen-user-trains` + `-next-id`), **negative auto-ids**, `subscribe`, CustomEvent `user-trains-changed` | `grid-actions/user-trains-store.js` |
| Projection-time merge hook | `prepareTrainData` concatenates user trains at **`schedule-renderer.js:363`** (verified) | `schedule-renderer.js` |
| User-record → train shape | `serviceLikeToTrain` (sets `userAdded`/`kind`) | `schedule-renderer.js:374` |
| Torn-paper split render | `expandSplitTrains` (verified: reads **only `train.splitTracks`**, min ≥45 px/half, both tracks rendered) + `decorateSplits` zigzag seam | `schedule-renderer.js:566`, `track-changes-decorator.js:229` |
| Live track-change suppression (indicator only) | **`TrackChangesStore.hide(trainId)`** — **only clears indicators for changes that already exist** (covers legs via the `entry.trainId===k` branch); a bare-id pre-emptive call is a no-op (see §5). The primary suppression is the `detectTrackChanges` gate, not this. | `track-changes/track-changes-store.js:122-134` |
| The poll that will fight you | `detectTrackChanges()` `forEach` (insertion point: immediately after `let mutated = false;` at `delay-integration.js:266`) | `delay-integration.js:247` |
| Non-drag menu entries | `TrainContextMenu.register({order, build})` + `createItem` | `train-context-menu-actions.js` |
| Toasts | `window.showNotification(msg, type)` (verified live) | global |
| Length / coupling validation | `calculateTrainSetLength`, `canBeMultiple`, `category` | `vehicleTypes.js` |
| Capacity / fit | `getTrackCapacityStatus`, `canTrainSetFitOnTrack` | `tracks.js` |
| Conflict re-check | `conflict-detector.detectConflicts` | `conflict-detector.js` |
| Reload/orphan surface | "Ladda om" banner | `schedule-update-checker.js` / `schedule-staleness-banner.js` |
| Regression gate | PDF-bar-accuracy harness | `verify/` (confirmed present) |

### Legacy vs active — what to avoid

- **Avoid:** `events/*`, `historyEngine.js`, `persistenceEngine.js`, `modalManager.js`, `menuSystem.js`, `emit_app_data.py` (older single-day emitter), the flat `initialServiceData` fallback. Reuse the `historyEngine` **action-type names only**, as vocabulary.
- **Active/authoritative:** the `js/modules/*` render pipeline (`schedule-renderer`, `train-renderer`, `train-positioning`, `day-stitcher`, `closure-renderer`), the `js/components/*` stores + decorators, `js/delay/*`, `emit_week_bundle.py` (the real week-bundle emitter). `trains.js` is auto-generated — **read-only, never mutate.**

---

## 4. Recommended UX design

### 4.1 Entry / exit — the pen verdict

**The pen-and-Slutför idea is correct; make it a hard, unmissable mode.** Clicking the pen (`✎ Redigera spår`) in the existing toolbar:

- `document.body.classList.add('is-editing')` — one class drives all CSS state. **No relayout** — bars stay exactly where they are; we only *reveal* affordances in place (this directly answers the "view→edit shift" friction the research warns about).
- Opens an in-memory **`EditSession`** (the command log; the immutable base *is* the snapshot, so we clone nothing).
- Renders persistent **edit chrome**: a left-docked **tool palette** and a bottom **action bar** — `● Redigerar — 3 ändringar · 1 konflikt   [Ångra] [Gör om] [Avbryt]  [Slutför ▸]`.
- **Widens the viewport cull** to the full 30 h for the session (today bars outside `viewTime ± timeRange/2 + 3h` aren't in the DOM — `schedule-renderer.js:633`) so a drag never crosses into un-rendered space. **Note the scope:** this widened cull is *session-only*. Committed edits persist and re-project on **every** later render, when the cull is back to the normal `viewTime ± (timeRange/2 + 3h)` window (`:629-638`, applied to `cachedTrains` *before* `expandSplitTrains`). That is fine because the **op-log projection runs against the full `cachedTrains` set** (the per-record cull happens later in `renderFullSchedule`, on `visibleTrains`, *after* projection), so an op whose `editKey` resolves to a currently-culled record is still applied/validated. The only thing the cull affects is whether the resulting bar is *drawn* this frame, which is correct. (See §6.3 for the exact projection ordering.)
- Sets `window.editSession.active = true` so `detectTrackChanges` suppresses its auto-switch mutation while a session is open (the feed keeps polling its store and keeps *detecting* divergence; it just won't yank a bar's track out from under you mid-edit; one reconcile pass runs on exit).

**Three explicit exit doors, never an implicit one:**
- **Slutför (commit):** final validation; if conflicts remain it does *not* silently block (controllers sometimes must commit reality) — a loud confirm "2 konflikter kvarstår. Slutför ändå?". On confirm, the session flattens into the persisted `TrainEditsStore` as one `BATCH_OPERATION`, fires `train-edits-changed`, removes the class, resumes live relayout.
- **Avbryt (discard):** confirm "Kasta 3 ändringar?" → clear log, re-render from base, zero residue.
- **Esc:** = Avbryt, but only after a confirm (guards against a fat-finger losing 20 minutes of work).

**Graft from inline-direct: a per-controller "🔒 Lås" look-only toggle** for a controller who wants to read with zero chance of arming an edit — cheap, and the inverse of a mode gate, so it costs nothing and adds a second safety valve.

### 4.2 The cut gesture

Activate scissors (palette or `C`). The cursor becomes a scissors; hovering a bar shows a **live vertical cut-guide** snapped to 5 min (`GridCoords.snapTime`). The *kind* of cut is **inferred from click position** (the third/third/middle zones in §2.2) so the controller is never forced through a "which kind?" menu; a long-press / right-click on the guide opens a 2-item disambiguation for the ambiguous case, so inference is never a trap.

**Critical correctness rule (and the basis for the zone inference):** the cut time is always computed from the record's `arrTime`/`depTime` **Dates** via the inverse of `left=(minFromStart/60)*pixelsPerHour`, **never** from `style.left/width` — bars are canvas-edge-clamped (`train-renderer.js:122-126`) so pixels lie for any overnight/edge bar. This is the same reason §2.2's zone test (left-third / middle / right-third) must be computed from `arrTime`/`depTime` positions rather than `rect.width`: a clamped bar's pixel width is not its logical span, so a third-of-pixel-width test would mis-zone near the canvas edges. `getBarMetrics` is fine for *proximity* hit-testing, never for deriving the logical cut time or the cut zone.

**Gating:** the scissors is enabled **only on `movementKind:'through'`** bars and **disabled at synthetic endpoints** (`arrSynthetic`/`depSynthetic` — those edges are canvas artifacts, not real movements; the guide refuses to snap there with a "Syntetisk kant — kan inte klippas" tooltip). For `stitchedOvernight` bars the cut resolves to the post-stitch span and persists against both underlying day-records (§6, §8).

### 4.3 The attach / join gesture

Activate link (palette or `A`). Grab a loose leg, drag it onto a target; **valid drop targets light up, everything else dims** (invalid-target feedback is mandatory per the Gantt research). The outcome is chosen by where you drop (§2.2 table).

**Graft from power-controller — keyboard attach is the headline accelerator and the a11y path for free:** select source → `J` enters a **target-pick state**; valid bars glow; `Tab`/`Shift+Tab` cycles candidates ranked by proximity; `Enter` commits. On a dense 196-bar board this is *faster and far more reliable* than dragging a loose tail, and it satisfies WCAG 2.5.7 with no extra work. `K` = couple (distinct verb).

### 4.4 Move / re-track / re-time

Two drag axes, distinct snapping, all geometry via `GridCoords`:

- **Vertical = re-track.** Snap to discrete `(trackId, subTrackIndex)` via `resolveTrack(y)`. Closed lanes (`SPARPLANEN_CLOSURES_WEEKS` + user closures) and over-occupied lanes highlight red and refuse the drop. **Lane is advisory** — `_packLanes` derives the final lane from time-overlap; the ghost shows the packer's actual resulting lane, mirroring the existing "delspår is a tie-breaker" design.
- **Horizontal = re-time.** 5-min snap; `Alt` bypasses for fine control. Edge handles resize arrival/departure independently with a `minDuration` clamp. Branch all math on `arrTime && depTime` vs single-ended (arrival-only fixed-80px left-anchored; departure-only right-anchored `left = depX − 80`).

**Graft from power-controller — the keyboard layer inside the mode** (the single change that neutralises mode-friction for fast repetitive work, and is *also* the WCAG non-drag path):
- **Number-key track-jump:** with a bar selected, press `1`–`9` (or `0`+digit for 10–16) to re-track it straight to that track. This is *the* power move.
- **Arrow nudge:** `←/→` = ±5-min re-time, `↑/↓` = ±1 lane. `[` / `]` select which edge resizes.

### 4.5 Snapping

Time snaps to 5 min (`GridCoords.SNAP_MINUTES`; grid lines are at 15 min so this gives sub-grid precision), plus edge-snap to neighbouring bars' start/end and to lane centres, respecting the **06:00 rollover / 30 h canvas** (`TimeManager`). Vertical snaps per track then per subTrack lane. `Alt` bypasses.

### 4.6 Tool palette & visual discoverability

Left-docked, **labelled icons** (never icon-only) with Swedish tooltips showing the single-key shortcut: `✎ Markera (V)`, `✂ Klipp (C)`, `🔗 Koppla (A)`, `↕ Flytta spår`, `↔ Ändra tid`, `🔧 Koppla/Dela enheter (K)`, `🗑 Ta bort`. The why-tooltips on validation ("Vändningstid under 10 min") teach the domain as the controller bumps into rules — better than a manual.

### 4.7 Visual language

| State | Treatment |
|---|---|
| **Base (untouched)** | Solid, PDF `barColor` — exactly as today |
| **Draft (this session, uncommitted)** | Dashed 2px border + `✎` corner glyph + slight inset — "mine, not yet real" (OpenTrack solid=committed/dashed=draft) |
| **Committed manual edit** | Solid + persistent **left accent rail** + `data-edit-locked` attribute the decorator reads to draw a manual badge (reuse the notes/checks badge slot) |
| **Conflict** | Red outline on *both* offending bars + count badge in the action bar, recomputed live via `conflict-detector` over the working set on every change |
| **Manual cut seam** | The existing torn-paper zigzag, **tinted distinctly** from the live-API split seam so "I cut this" reads differently from "the feed split this" |
| **Live-data (untouched)** | The existing arrow/ghost/tear-seam — **dimmed to ~40%** while `is-editing` so the draft dominates |

Distinction is **redundant** (shape + glyph + colour accent, never colour alone) — colour-blind safe on an already colour-coded board.

### 4.8 Touch + mouse + pen, accessibility & reduced-motion

- **Pointer Events + `setPointerCapture(pointerId)`** for one unified path (you explicitly mentioned pen). Handle `pointercancel` **symmetrically** to `pointerup` or state leaks. `touch-action:none` on `.train-bar` **only in edit mode** so touch-drag doesn't scroll the `.schedule-wrapper`. Read pointer coords rect-relative (`getBoundingClientRect`), matching `GridCoords`, since `canvas.scrollLeft` is always 0. **Do not** copy the dead document-level mousemove approach (capture-less, touch-broken).
- **WCAG 2.5.7 (Level AA):** every drag has a non-drag twin from day one. The keyboard layer (§4.4) *is* that path; additionally register `TrainContextMenu` entries "Flytta till spår…", "Ändra ankomst/avgång…", "Klipp här", "Koppla till tåg…", "Återställ". Make `.train-bar` focusable (`tabindex=0`, `aria-label` with numbers + track); Enter/Space opens the menu.
- **Reduced-motion (a documented landmine on your work machines — animation-dependent UI vanishes):** every affordance — handles, cut seam, draft border, drag ghost, snap line, the Redigerar bar, the suppression toast — must be visible via **static CSS with a fully-visible default end-state**. Any nicety (snap pulse, commit flash) is wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` only. Verify the static end-state explicitly.

### 4.9 Keyboard alternatives (summary)

`E` toggles mode · `C`/`Shift+C` cut · `A`/`J` attach-turn · `K` couple · `1–9`/`0+digit` track-jump · `←/→` ±5-min, `Shift+←/→` ±1-min · `↑/↓` lane · `[`/`]` edge select · `⌘Z`/`⌘⇧Z` undo/redo · `Esc` exit · `?` keymap card.

---

## 5. The track-change-suppression question — deep analysis & recommendation

This is your explicit requirement and the most opinionated call in the design. There are four candidate models:

1. **Pin/lock (manual override wins, feed stops mutating)** — the manual track is authoritative; the poll skips the auto-switch for the train.
2. **Suppress-indicator only** (`TrackChangesStore.hide`) — hides the arrow/ghost but the feed still mutates `trackId` on the next poll.
3. **Badge-as-overridden** — show the bar as "operator-set", but let the feed keep flowing.
4. **Pause-live** (globally freeze the feed while editing) — blunt; throws away the delay/conflict information that *drives* the edits.

**Recommendation: a composite of (1) + (2) + (3) — pin-and-override-with-an-escape-hatch — and explicitly reject (4) and reject silent suppression.** The reconciliation research is unanimous: a disagreement must be a *visible, dismissible chip*, never silent last-write-wins.

Why suppress-indicator alone (model 2) is insufficient and a trap: `detectTrackChanges()` runs every 30 s, compares API `trackAtLocation` against `plannedTrackId`, and (auto-switch on) overwrites `train.trackId` or sets `splitTracks`, then forces a re-render. It even *folds a bar back to `plannedTrackId`* when announcements age out (`clearSplit`). **A manual re-track is silently reverted within 30 seconds unless the loop is gated.** This is the highest-priority wiring in the whole feature.

> **Why `hide()` cannot be the suppression mechanism (load-bearing).** It is tempting to "pre-emptively" call `TrackChangesStore.hide(train.id)` when the controller re-tracks a *clean* train and trust it to cover all legs. **It does not.** Verified at `track-changes-store.js:122-134`: `hide()` only suppresses a record's legs through its `entry.trainId === k` branch, which requires matching change entries to *already exist* in the store. On a clean manual override — the dominant case, since the whole point is the API has *not* reported a divergence — the internal `filter` returns empty, `hide()` takes the `matching.length === 0` path and just adds the bare `train.id` string to the `hidden` Set. That orphan never matches anything: `getActive()` (`:107`) and `isHidden()` (`:148-149`) test the composite `changeId` `${train.id}:${leg}:${trainNumber}`, never the bare id; and `recordChange()`'s auto-unhide (`:86`) only deletes `k` when `k === changeId`, so the orphan **leaks forever**. **Therefore: the gate in `detectTrackChanges` (step 1) is the real suppression; `hide()` is only an indicator-clear for changes that already exist, and is called *after* a change is recorded (step 2).**

**The concrete mechanism, in order:**

1. **Stop the feed *mutating* you — but keep it *detecting*.** Insert the `manualOverride` guard **immediately after `let mutated = false;`** in `detectTrackChanges` — verified line **`delay-integration.js:266`**, *before* the `const planned = …` line — so it precedes `clearSplit` (called at `:283` and `:290`), the first `recordChange` (`:295`), and the auto-switch (`:317`). **This guard is *not* a blanket early-return.** A blanket return would defeat the design's other half: a genuinely-later API divergence (the API reports a *third* track, different from even your manual choice) would never be computed, because the early-return skips the very compare that surfaces it. Instead the guard **runs the API-vs-`plannedTrackId` compare** (so divergence is detectable) but **gates only the mutation/auto-switch/`clearSplit`/`recordChange` branch**: when `manualOverride` is set and the API agrees with the plan or with your manual track, do nothing; when the API reports a *new* third track, route that to the **"Live disagrees" chip** (step 3) instead of mutating `trackId`/`splitTracks`. *Do not* mirror the `allHidden` guard's position — that guard is at `:335`, far below `clearSplit`, and gates the opposite concern (folding a hidden bar's split back); placing the override guard there would let the poll fold your manual track back before the guard ever runs.
2. **Clear any *existing* visible indicator** with **`TrackChangesStore.hide(train.id)` — but only *after* a change has actually been recorded** (or via a new per-train `suppress(trainId)`; see below). On a clean override there is no change entry, so `hide()` would be a no-op and is skipped. When a change *does* exist (e.g. the API had already flagged a divergence before the controller overrode), `hide()` correctly clears all its legs via the `entry.trainId===k` branch. **Recommended hardening:** extend `TrackChangesStore` with a per-train `suppress(trainId)` that `getActive()`/`isHidden()` honor **by `trainId`** even for changes that don't yet exist — i.e. test `suppressed.has(entry.trainId)` alongside the composite-`changeId` check. That makes the suppression robust against a change the poll records 30 s later, without depending on the gate alone. (The `detectTrackChanges` gate already prevents the *mutation*; `suppress(trainId)` additionally keeps the *indicator* from ever flashing for that train.)
3. **`plannedTrackId` policy: freeze it, don't overwrite.** Keep `plannedTrackId` as the original plan and add a `manualOverride` flag. Rationale: if the API *later* reports a track diverging from *even your manual choice*, that's genuinely new information you should see — surfaced as a dismissible **"Live disagrees" chip** ("Live säger spår 7, du valde spår 5") via the **Följ live igen** affordance — rather than silently erased. This is exactly why the step-1 guard gates the *mutation*, not the *detection*: the compare must still run for this chip to ever fire. Overwriting `plannedTrackId` would blind the board to real future divergence.
4. **Dismissible toast** via `window.showNotification`: **"Live spårändringar avstängda för tåg 344 — Följ live igen"** with one-click relink. Record `who/when` on the op for audit.

> **Latent store bug the feature interacts with (flag, don't depend on).** `TrackChangesStore`'s auto-prune window is computed as `Math.max(15, minutes) * 60 * 1000` — the `15` reads as an intended 15-*second* floor but is applied to *minutes*, so the documented ~2-min window actually behaves as **~15 minutes**. This is a latent bug, but it touches this feature directly: the "Live disagrees" chip and the suppression toast must **not** inherit that auto-prune. The **manual-override badge is a persistent flag on the op/projection, not a `TrackChangesStore` entry**, so it does *not* expire on the store's (mis-clamped) timer — make this explicit in implementation so the edit badge never silently disappears after ~15 minutes. Only the *live* arrow/ghost lifetime is governed by that clamp; the manual badge outlives it by design.

**The "don't show again" idea — verdict: yes, but only on the toast, never on the suppression itself.** Suppression must always be visible via the persistent badge/lock glyph; only the *notification* gets a per-session "Visa inte igen". A black-box suppression where the controller can't tell a train was detached from live is an operational hazard.

**Scope of "turn off":** the default detaches **track-changes** (your literal words → the `detectTrackChanges` mutation gate + `splitTracks` skip + `TrackChangesStore` indicator suppression). Delay/cancellation overlays (`suppressedDelays`) stay *on* unless the controller also explicitly slopar the train — two separate levers, matching the two existing context-menu items. **Do not pause live globally** — the feed is the information driving the edits.

**Graft from inline-direct — the live-coupling re-derivation:** after any number/time edit, re-derive the bar's delay context via `getDelayContextsForTrain` with the new numbers/times, recompute `dayOffset` if the edit crosses the 06:00 rollover, and surface a one-line **"Förseningsdata frikopplad / live-koppling uppdaterad"** chip — because the ±5-min `advertisedTime` guard means a leg moved >5 min from its announcement makes `getDelayInfo` return `null` and the delay overlay *silently vanishes*. The chip turns a mysterious disappearance into an explained state.

---

## 6. Persistence & live-data reconciliation

### 6.1 Stable identity — the central constraint

Record `id` is **positional and renumbers every weekly regen** (`enumerate(...,1)` in `emit_week_bundle.py`), so id 5 next week is a different physical train. The existing notes/checks stores key by `String(train.id)` and **already orphan silently every week** — confirm this with the owner, because the new key resolver should be retrofitted onto them. Stitched bars get a runtime `d1-<id>`. **Every edit must key on a stable composite business key**, mirroring how `delay-data-manager`/`getDelayInfo` already match on (number + time), not id:

```
editKey = `${weekKey}|${dayKey}|${plannedTrackId}|${subTrackIndex}|${arrivalTrainNumber}@${arrHH:MM}|${departureTrainNumber}@${depHH:MM}`
```

- **`subTrackIndex` is part of the canonical key** (added above). It is required so two numberless operation moves that share track+label+time but sit on different sub-lanes don't collide — the operation-bar fallback already relies on it, and omitting it from the main template was an inconsistency.
- **`plannedTrackId`-freeze is a load-bearing prerequisite for key stability across a re-track.** A manual re-track — the headline MVP operation — changes `trackId`, but the key uses `plannedTrackId`, which §5 step 3 **freezes**. The key therefore survives a re-track *only because* `plannedTrackId` is frozen. State this dependency explicitly: **any op that would change `plannedTrackId` must re-key (emit a new `editKey` and migrate the op) or is forbidden.** If a future op silently rewrites `plannedTrackId`, the key rebinds to a different bar — a safety hazard. The freeze is not just a live-feed convenience; it is what keeps the persistence key stable.
- **Departure-only bars:** use the departure twin (`d${departureTrainNumber}@${depHH:MM}`).
- **Arrival-only / operation (label-only D/MVH/SÄR) bars:** fall back to `(track, subTrack, label, arrTime)` — they have **no train number** and an empty number must **never** participate in the key (it would collide across every numberless bar on a track; `getDelayInfo` also returns null on blank numbers). Mirror `user-trains-store.normalize`'s `String().trim()`.
- `record.id` is kept only as a **soft hint** for fast same-session matching.
- `dayOffset` is folded in for `stitchedOvernight` bars.

### 6.2 The edits store shape

New file `js/components/edit-mode/train-edits-store.js`, a carbon copy of the `UserTrainsStore` IIFE (verified keys: localStorage `sparplannen-train-edits` + `-next-id`, **negative ids**, `subscribe`, CustomEvent `train-edits-changed`). It stores an **append-only op log, not mutated records**:

```js
{ id: <neg>,
  op: 'cut'|'attach'|'retrack'|'retime'|'couple'|'split'|'delete',
  editKey,
  params: { newTrackId, newSubTrack, cutTime, mode, newDepartureNumber, ... },
  before, after,          // AUDIT/DIAGNOSTIC snapshot ONLY — not the undo mechanism (see §9)
  scope: { weekKey, dayKey, anchorStr },
  createdAt, createdBy }   // audit trail
```

The **op + params** drive everything: the projection (§6.3) and undo (§9) both derive purely from the op log. The `before`/`after` field snapshot is kept **only as an audit/diagnostic record** — it is *not* consulted by undo, which works by popping the last op and re-projecting (§9). This removes the earlier ambiguity where both `op/params` and `before/after` looked like they could drive undo: **only `op/params` do.** The append-only log gives **free undo** (pop + re-project) and **free persistence** (localStorage).

### 6.3 Overlay / projection — never mutate the base

Apply the op log as a **projection inside `prepareTrainData`**. To remove the earlier sequence ambiguity, here is the exact order `prepareTrainData` runs:

1. **Resolve base service records** for the selected `week`/`day`.
2. **`DayStitcher.stitch(...)`** — merges genuine overnight pairs into single records in **service-record space** (this runs *inside* `prepareTrainData`'s resolve step, *not* "after :363").
3. **Map** each resolved/stitched service record → a `train` render object (parses `HH:MM`→Date, sets `arrSynthetic`/`depSynthetic`, `plannedTrackId`, `movementKind`, `dayOffset`).
4. **Merge user-added trains** at the `mergedTrains = trainData.concat(...)` line — verified **`schedule-renderer.js:363`** — in **mapped-train-object space** (this is the user-trains concat, which happens *after* stitching).
5. **Project the op log** here, immediately after the `:363` merge: resolve each op's `editKey` against the merged train objects and apply its transform to the **projected `cachedTrains` copy only**.

So the projection runs in **train-object space (after `:363`, after stitching)**, *not* in service-record space. This matters for `stitchedOvernight` bars: an edit on a stitched bar operates on the already-merged runtime bar (with its `_dayOffset`/`d1-<id>`), and persists against the *underlying day-record(s)* via the natural key (§8), never the runtime `d1-` id. (The earlier draft's "after :363 **and** after DayStitcher" phrasing was not contradictory once unpacked — stitching is step 2, the `:363` concat is step 4 — but the sequence is now stated explicitly so an implementer can't read it two ways.)

Because the projection touches **only the projected copy**, base `trains.js` stays read-only, weekly regen keeps working, and **re-running `prepareTrainData` = re-applying edits = free coherence** (the render is a full teardown+rebuild with no diff, so a projection-time overlay costs nothing). The per-record viewport cull happens *later*, in `renderFullSchedule` on `visibleTrains` (`:629-638`), so projection always sees the **full** set — an op whose `editKey` resolves to a currently-culled record is still applied and validated; the cull only decides what's drawn this frame (§4.1). Hook a `train-edits-changed` listener next to the existing `user-trains-changed` one (`schedule-renderer.js:68`); decorators re-attach on `schedule:rendered` automatically.

**Optimistic edits are free** because overlays decorate the projected copy and never the base — the edit shows instantly on the single post-commit render.

### 6.4 Multi-user Firestore model

**Ship localStorage-first** — it matches every existing store, and the app is single-device today (Firestore is *only* the create-only `onskemal` feedback channel; rules deny-all otherwise). Be explicit with the owner that edits are device-local for now. **Phase 2:** one Firestore doc per op, **last-write-wins-with-history** (keep the loser undoable — per-field merge/CRDT is overkill for a handful of controllers), a new collection + relaxed rules, and a **visible dismissible reconcile chip** on disagreement — never silent last-write-wins.

### 6.5 Orphan handling on refresh

On each load, **re-resolve every op's `editKey`** against current records. `resolveEditKey()` returns the matching records **and a match COUNT**; **any count ≠ 1 is surfaced, never auto-applied.** Four buckets:

- **matched (count = 1, exact)** — apply.
- **shifted (count = 1, fuzzy within ±5 min** on a renumbered/moved record — requires a glance) — surface to the tray for one-click confirm.
- **ambiguous (count > 1)** — the key matched **multiple** current records (a weekly regen can renumber a numberless operation bar's fuzzy fallback key into two candidates; a re-paired turn can now share numbers with another bar). Goes to the reconcile tray with a **manual pick** — the controller chooses which record the edit binds to. **Never auto-apply to >1 match.**
- **orphaned (count = 0, gone** — e.g. a cancelled train) — surface with keep-mine / drop choices.

Shifts, ambiguous, and orphans all go to a **tray hung off the existing "Ladda om" banner**. **Never auto-delete; never auto-apply to a mismatched or ambiguous bar** — a silent re-bind to the wrong train is a safety hazard. Extract a shared `resolveEditKey()` util (returning `{ matches: [...], count }`) and retrofit it onto the notes/checks stores.

---

## 7. Feature set, prioritised

| Tier | Feature | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Must-have (MVP)** | Explicit edit mode (pen → Slutför/Avbryt/Esc); no relayout on toggle | The safety envelope; the audit/undo/feed-freeze boundary |
| | **Re-track** via drag + number-key jump + `↑/↓` | The single most frequent controller decision at Göteborg C |
| | **`manualOverride` mutation-gate in `detectTrackChanges`** (compare still runs) + per-train indicator suppression + dismissible toast | Without the gate the feature is broken within 30 s; the gate (not `hide()`) is the real suppression |
| | `TrainEditsStore` op log, stable `editKey` (incl. `subTrackIndex`), projection at `:363` | The persistence backbone; survives weekly regen |
| | Context-menu twins + focusable bars (WCAG 2.5.7) | Legally required; also more precise on a dense board |
| | In-session undo/redo (`⌘Z`) via op-log re-projection | Every real editor; makes Avbryt trivial |
| | Static, reduced-motion-safe visuals | Documented vanishing-UI bug on the owner's machines |
| **Should-have** | **Re-time** (edge-resize + `←/→` nudge) with snap + `dayOffset` recompute across 06:00 | Second-most-common move; needs overnight care |
| | **Cut** (sever-turn + time-split) reusing generalised `editSplit||splitTracks` render + `_splitSibling` flag | The literal "cut" ask |
| | Live conflict re-paint via `conflict-detector` on every edit | Shows the controller when a re-track creates an overlap |
| | Live-coupling re-derivation chip | Explains a vanishing delay overlay |
| | Orphan reconcile tray (matched/shifted/ambiguous/orphaned) on the reload banner | Edits never silently corrupt across weeks |
| **Nice-to-have** | **Attach** (form turn `J` / couple `K`) with target-pick Tab-cycle + soft validation | The "attach to others" ask; higher complexity |
| | Auto `kind:'shunting'` växling connector on cross-track re-pair | Keeps the board physically plausible |
| | "🔒 Lås" look-only toggle | Cheap second safety valve |
| | Per-track platform length added to `tracks.js` | Makes the length check *real* (advisory until then) |
| **Probably-not (defer / out of scope)** | Command palette `⌘K`, multi-select, `BATCH` batch-move | Front-loads scope that competes with shipping the core safely; revisit once the mode+commit model is proven |
| | Firestore multi-user sync | Net-new collection + relaxed rules + reconcile; clean phase 2, not phase 1 |
| | Per-field CRDT merge | Overkill for a few controllers |

---

## 8. Edge cases & failure modes

Handling is woven into §§4–7 above; this table is the consolidated reference.

| Case | Severity | Handling |
|---|---|---|
| **Synthetic endpoint** (`arrSynthetic`/`depSynthetic` — 00:00 clamp, `_syntheticDepFar`, stitch marker) | High | Disable cut handle / attach target on the synthetic end; "Syntetisk kant — kan inte klippas"; refuse to fabricate a departure from a synthetic number |
| **`stitchedOvernight` bar** (`d1-<id>`, two day-records, no single source row) | **Blocker** | Resolve to post-stitch bar; persist against the *correct underlying day-record* via the natural key, never the `d1-` runtime id; recompute `dayOffset`/`dateHint` after any time edit crossing 06:00 |
| **`continuesFromPrev/ToNextPage`** (3 h *page* edge, not a day boundary) | High | Treat as "may be a fragment"; always resolve to the fully-stitched runtime bar before offering a cut; never expose the page edge as a cut anchor |
| **Weekly-regen orphaning** (ids renumber) | **Blocker** | Stable composite `editKey` (incl. `subTrackIndex`); `resolveEditKey()` returns a match **count**; re-resolve into matched/shifted/**ambiguous**/orphaned each load; tray on reload banner; **count ≠ 1 is never auto-applied** |
| **OTN/ATN divergence** (~8%, 344↔10344) + re-pair breaking the ±5-min match | High | The `detectTrackChanges` gate + per-train indicator suppression cover the legs (no dual-index needed for suppression); but for *delay re-matching* after a re-pair, carry the new leg's `scheduledTime` within ±5 min of the new number's `advertisedTime`, else warn "live-koppling uppdaterad"; treat post-edit `null` delayInfo as "link broken", not "on time" |
| **Live `detectTrackChanges` poll stomping a manual re-track** | **Blocker** | `manualOverride` guard **immediately after `let mutated = false;` at `delay-integration.js:266`**, before `clearSplit` (`:283`/`:290`), `recordChange` (`:295`) and auto-switch (`:317`); **gate the mutation branch, not the compare** (so genuine later divergence still surfaces, §5); freeze `plannedTrackId`; dismissible toast. *Not* placed at the `allHidden` guard (`:335`) — that is below `clearSplit` and serves a different purpose |
| **Manual cut vs live `splitTracks`** | High | Different-track split → generalise `expandSplitTrains` to honour `editSplit || splitTracks` (one `||`), flagged manual so the poll skips it; **same-track temporal cut → two real overlay records carrying a shared `_splitSibling`/`editDerived` flag** (the torn renderer only encodes different-track and the API path *never* produces `arrivalTrack===departureTrack` — this is the genuine render gap a manual cut must fill). See the dedicated row below for the downstream-aggregator handling that flag drives; respect the ≥45 px/half + both-tracks-rendered gate |
| **Same-track split feeding downstream aggregators** | **Blocker** | A same-track temporal cut is the *one* edit that changes the record **count** (one bar → two synthetic same-track records at adjacent times), and every count-sensitive aggregator must treat the pair as **one logical occupancy** via the shared `_splitSibling`/`editDerived` flag: **`conflict-detector`** must suppress the mutual self-overlap (two same-track records at adjacent times would otherwise register as a double-occupation); **`_packLanes`** must treat the pair as one occupant so it doesn't allocate two competing lanes and re-flow neighbours; **`workload-aggregator`** must count the leg **once** (it already special-cases `arrSynthetic`/`depSynthetic` — mirror that); **`getDelayContextsForTrain`** must run **once** for the shared physical movement, not twice. Without the flag, a same-track cut double-counts workload, fabricates a phantom conflict, and re-packs the track |
| **Closures / inactive region as drop target** | Medium | Check destination against `SPARPLANEN_CLOSURES_WEEKS` + user closures + inactive-region bounds; highlight invalid, warn "Spår avstängt" (soft block, overridable) |
| **Split-bar / synthetic bars feeding downstream** | High | Validate every committed record against the exact shape `prepareTrainData` produces; re-run `conflict-detector` + `updateAllVisualizations` after each edit (workload-aggregator special-cases `arrSynthetic`/`depSynthetic`; same-track siblings carry `_splitSibling` — see row above) |
| **Perf / transition storm** | High | Drag mutates **only the dragged div** via `transform: translate()`, never `left/top/width`; `renderFullSchedule` once on drop, never per-frame; keep draft styling on explicit transition props, never `transition:all` |
| **Reduced-motion** | High | Every affordance has a static visible end-state; niceties gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference`; verify the end-state explicitly |
| **Zero/negative-duration cut** (renderer returns null → bar vanishes) | High | Enforce min dwell ≥ one snap step (ideally ≥ ~10-min vändningstid floor) and reject `depTime ≤ arrTime` **at commit**, not just at render — a dropped bar is silent data loss |
| **Vehicle-type / turnaround / coupling invariants** | Medium | **Soft** validations with Swedish messaging (controllers override reality): "Vändningstid under X min", "Fordonstyperna kan inte multipelkopplas", count-conservation on split/join. Disable couple/split for `canBeMultiple:false` (X2/SJ3000). Length check **advisory** ("Längdkontroll preliminär") until `tracks.js` carries platform length and `vehicleTypeID` stops being hard-coded X40 |
| **Arrival-only / departure-only / operation halves** | High | Gate cut on `movementKind==='through'`; single-ended bars allow only re-track/re-time/attach; branch all geometry on `arrTime && depTime`; label-only bars use a track+subTrack+time+label key |
| **Refresh mid-edit** (hard `window.location.reload()` wipes all in-memory state) | High | Persist committed edits to localStorage *before* any reload; detect an in-progress session and defer/confirm or auto-commit-then-reload, never silently discard; re-resolve after reload |
| **TrackChangesStore duration clamp** (`Math.max(15, minutes)` → ~15 min, not ~2 min) | Low (latent) | Known store bug the feature *interacts* with but must not depend on: the manual-override badge and "Live disagrees" chip use a **persistent op/projection flag**, *not* a `TrackChangesStore` entry, so they do **not** inherit the ~15-min auto-prune. Only the live arrow/ghost lifetime is governed by the clamp |
| **Multi-user concurrency** | Medium | localStorage-first, explicitly device-local; phase-2 Firestore LWW-with-history + visible reconcile chip |
| **Cut from clamped pixels** | High | Always compute cut time *and the cut zone* (§2.2 thirds) from `arrTime`/`depTime` Dates via `GridCoords`, never `style.left/width` — clamped/overnight bars make pixel width lie |
| **Couple vs turn conflated** | Medium | Distinct verbs (`K` raises `count`, re-validate length; `J` rewrites departure number only); **test the renderer draws an operator-raised `count`** — `count` has only ever come from the extractor |

---

## 9. Undo/redo, history & safety nets

**Do not revive `historyEngine.js`** — dead, AppState-coupled, and its full-array `deepClone` snapshots are wrong for 196 bars. **Reuse its action-type *names* as the `op` vocabulary only.**

**The op log is the single source of truth for undo — there is no separate command stack.** The earlier draft described two incompatible models (an append-only projection *and* a `{before, after}` command stack); this resolves it to **one**:

- The `EditSession` holds the **ordered op log** (`{op, editKey, params}` entries, §6.2), keyed by the stable `editKey` (not the volatile id).
- **`undo()` = pop the last op off the log + re-run `prepareTrainData`'s projection** (§6.3). **`redo()` = push the popped op back + re-project.** Because the projection is **re-applied on every render**, there is no separate "apply `before`" step — you re-derive the board by replaying the *remaining* ops over the immutable base. This is the whole reason the op-log model was chosen: undo is free.
- **`before`/`after` are an audit/diagnostic snapshot only** (§6.2) — they are *never* consulted to perform an undo. (The previous "`undo()` applies `before`, `redo()` applies `after`" language is removed; it described a mutate-in-place command stack incompatible with a pure projection.)
- **Scoped to the open session**, bound to `⌘Z`/`⌘⇧Z` **only while `is-editing`** — no global undo on a live board. `Avbryt` = "pop all ops back to session start + re-project" is then trivial. A new edit clears the redo tail (standard); cap log length.
- A multi-bar op is one `BATCH_OPERATION` entry so one undo (one pop + re-project) reverts the whole batch.
- **Confirmation/discard:** Slutför with conflicts → loud confirm; Avbryt/Esc → "Kasta N ändringar?".
- **Audit log:** Slutför flattens the session into one persisted `BATCH_OPERATION` with `createdBy`/timestamp — one audit entry per session, and a clean "revert this whole session" later. This is dispatch best practice (manual override is first-class *and audited*). The `before`/`after` field snapshots live here, in the audit record, exactly where a diagnostic "what did this op change" view needs them — and nowhere else.
- **Safety nets:** drag-threshold before a move arms (a stray click never nudges); commit-before-reload; never rely on the renderer's null-return to "handle" a zero-width bar.

---

## 10. Phased implementation roadmap

Each phase is independently shippable and validated against the `verify/` PDF-bar-accuracy harness (and the console fake-delay-feed trick from MEMORY for the poll interaction).

**Phase 1 — Mode + re-track + the live truce (the core that proves the model).**
*New:* `js/components/edit-mode/{edit-mode-controller, train-edit-interactions, train-edits-store, train-edits-decorator}.js`, `styles/components/edit-mode.css`.
*Touch:* `schedule-renderer.js` (apply op log after the `:363` merge, in train-object space per §6.3; widen cull when `is-editing`; `train-edits-changed` listener at `:68`); `delay-integration.js` (`manualOverride` **mutation-gate** immediately after `let mutated = false;` at `:266` — the compare still runs, §5); `track-changes-store.js` (add per-train `suppress(trainId)` honored by `getActive`/`isHidden`; reuse `hide` only for already-recorded changes); `train-context-menu-actions.js` (non-drag twins); `index.html` (script tags near `:966`).
Delivers: move a strip to another track (drag + number-key + `↑/↓`), context-menu twins, suppression toast, in-session undo via re-projection. **Proves the feed truce — the highest-risk wiring.**

**Phase 2 — Re-time.** Edge-resize + `←/→` nudge with snap; `dayOffset` recompute across 06:00; min-dwell + inverted-duration rejection at commit. *Touch:* `train-edit-interactions.js`, `train-edits-store.js`.

**Phase 3 — Cut.** Generalise `expandSplitTrains` to `editSplit || splitTracks`; add the **same-track two-record** representation **with the shared `_splitSibling`/`editDerived` flag** and teach `conflict-detector`, `_packLanes`, `workload-aggregator`, and `getDelayContextsForTrain` to honor it (§8); scissors gesture + "Klipp här" menu twin; synthetic/page-flag/stitched guards; zone inference from `arrTime`/`depTime`, not `rect.width`. *Touch:* `schedule-renderer.js` (`expandSplitTrains:566`), `conflict-detector.js`, `train-positioning.js` (`_packLanes`), `workload-aggregator.js`, `delay-integration.js` (`getDelayContextsForTrain`), interactions, store. Re-paint conflicts live.

**Phase 4 — Attach.** `J` form-turn (target-pick Tab-cycle) + `K` couple; soft validation (`calculateTrainSetLength`, `category`/`canBeMultiple`); live-coupling re-derivation chip; optional växling connector. *Touch:* interactions, store, `vehicleTypes.js`/`tracks.js` reads.

**Phase 5 (deferred) — Multi-user + power surface.** Firestore LWW-with-history + reconcile chip; then (optionally) command palette / multi-select / batch once the core is proven.

---

## 11. Open questions for the owner — with recommended defaults

1. **Same-track temporal cut representation.** The torn renderer only encodes different-track; a same-track cut has no existing visual. → **Default: two real adjacent overlay records carrying a shared `_splitSibling`/`editDerived` flag** (not a faked `splitTracks`), rendered as two normal bars but treated as **one logical occupancy** by `conflict-detector`, `_packLanes`, `workload-aggregator`, and `getDelayContextsForTrain` (§8) so the second record doesn't fabricate a conflict, steal a lane, or double-count workload. Confirm this reads acceptably on the board.
2. **`plannedTrackId` on manual re-track: freeze or rewrite?** → **Default: freeze** + `manualOverride`. Two reasons, both load-bearing: (a) a genuinely-later API divergence surfaces as a "Live disagrees" chip (the `detectTrackChanges` gate runs the *compare* even while suppressing the *mutation*, §5); and (b) the `editKey` uses `plannedTrackId`, so freezing it is what keeps the persistence key stable across the re-track (§6.1). Rewriting would both blind the board to future spårändring *and* silently rebind the edit key.
3. **Retrofit the stable-key resolver onto notes/checks stores** (which orphan silently every week today)? → **Default: yes**, as part of Phase 1's `resolveEditKey()` util (which returns a match count, §6.5) — it's a latent data-loss bug regardless of this feature.
4. **Does an operator-raised `trainSet.count` render as a longer multi-unit bar?** `count` has only ever come from the extractor. → **Default: test in Phase 4 before enabling couple**; if the renderer needs an explicit multi-unit model, scope that as a couple-specific follow-up.
5. **Are edits device-local or shared between controllers?** → **Default: device-local (localStorage)** for Phase 1–4; Firestore sync is a clean Phase 5. The owner's "controllers" wording implies shared state that does not exist today — confirm scope before designing the collection.
6. **Min-dwell / vändningstid floor as a hard block or soft warn?** → **Default: soft warn** (~10 min commuter) per controllers-override-reality, but **hard-reject `depTime ≤ arrTime`** and zero-width at commit (that's data loss, not a judgment call).
7. **Should "Slutför with unresolved conflicts" be allowed?** → **Default: yes, behind a loud confirm** — dispatchers sometimes must commit reality-as-it-is; blocking would be wrong, but it must be unmissable.
8. **`TrackChangesStore` duration clamp (`Math.max(15, minutes)` → ~15 min).** Worth fixing the latent clamp bug while we're in this file? → **Default: leave the clamp alone for now but do NOT let edit badges depend on it** — the manual-override badge/chip is a persistent op/projection flag, not a store entry, so it already survives the mis-clamped prune (§5, §8). Flag the clamp to the owner as a separate one-line fix.
